data.body:
PASS 1 (G6 — approach soundness) review of Cluster 2 (unify the two `_advisory_lock` backoff functions):

No findings for G6. Affirmations:

- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS [G6]: Verified `_compute_backoff_seconds` (`_advisory_lock.py:126-137`) and `_cas_backoff_seconds` (`_advisory_lock.py:417-429`) are structurally identical, differing only in the four constants. A single `_jittered_backoff(retry_index, *, base, factor, cap, jitter_fraction)` reproduces both exactly. The plan's explicit invariant — "exactly one `random.uniform` call (preserving the RNG draw count/order, so a seeded sequence is identical)" — is the correct correctness constraint: both originals issue exactly one `uniform` draw, so seeded sequences stay byte-identical. Pure-function refactor; no edge/empty/concurrent/ordering/atomicity hazard.

- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE [G6]: Solves the named problem (collapse the one genuine in-file duplication) directly, not a proxy. Thin wrappers keep both call sites (`acquire_pass_lock` line 260, `_cas_advance_with_retry` line 464) and both budgets (`_LOCK_RETRY_BUDGET_DEFAULT`, `_CAS_RETRY_BUDGET`) unchanged.

- APPROACH SELECTION [G6]: Generated alternatives — (a) a universal `retry()`/`_retry.py` helper, (b) inlining math at call sites, (c) dropping wrappers and passing constants directly. (a) is explicitly scoped out by AC with a sound rationale (other retry sites solve different problems → left bespoke); narrower scope = smaller blast-radius + better codebase-alignment. (b) re-introduces duplication (strictly worse). (c) is permitted by the plan but keeping wrappers preserves call-site/test stability. Chosen approach is defensibly at-least-as-good on all axes; no clearly-superior missed alternative.

- POSITIVE RATIONALE [G6]: Present in the "Why" section ("One backoff formula in one place is observable and tunable; two hand-copied copies drift silently") plus a behavior-preservation rationale.

Cross-check: the referenced jitter test (`tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_advisory_lock_retry_jitter.py`) asserts STRUCTURAL backoff invariants rather than pinned constants, so it will tolerate the unified helper as the plan anticipates.
